posts.text:
🍜 NEW: This hidden ramen shop in Tanjong Pagar has THE most rich tonkotsu broth we've had in SG. Queue starts at 11am sharp. Get the special with black garlic oil 🧄

Kano Ramen
📍 Tanjong Pagar Plaza #01-07
⏰ 11am–3pm, 5pm–9pm (closed Mon)

Tried this new chili crab pasta place at Telok Ayer and honestly it slaps. Fusion done right. The crab is generous and the pasta absorbs all that sweet, spicy sauce. ~$28/pax.

📍 Telok Ayer Street, near Thian Hock Keng

Finally found the best wanton mee in the East! Uncle has been doing this for 40 years and you can tell. Springy noodles, crispy char siu, flavourful soup. Only $4.50!

📍 Bedok North Ave 1 Blk 538, #01-660
⏰ 6am–1pm daily

Omakase under $100? This kaiseki counter at Keong Saik is quietly one of the best-value Japanese meals in Singapore. 8 courses, incredibly curated ingredients. Book at least 2 weeks ahead.

📍 Keong Saik Road

Weekend warrior eats: the laksa at this stall in Katong is legitimately world-class. Thick coconut gravy, whole prawns, cockles — the works. Got here at 10am and it was already packed.

328 Katong Laksa
📍 51 E Coast Rd

New brunch spot alert at Dempsey! Their eggs benedict with pulled pork is incredible — the hollandaise is silky smooth and not too heavy. Great for a lazy Sunday. Prices are reasonable for the area ~$20–30/pax.

📍 Block 10 Dempsey Road

Underrated gem: the beef noodles at this Hong Kong-style cha chaan teng at Novena. Braised beef shank in a rich, dark broth with silky flat noodles. $9.80. Come before 12 or you'll wait 30 min.

📍 Thomson Road, near Novena MRT
